danger_signals.visual_form: Demons appear continuously on screen in varied patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Unlimited threat; player cannot clear all enemies, must navigate around them

danger_signals.visual_form: Orange/red animated fireballs moving in arcs or spinning patterns
danger_signals.meaning: Instant death hazard; any contact with demons or their projectiles kills Dana

danger_signals.visual_form: Visible timer display decreasing during level progression
danger_signals.meaning: Time pressure; level failure if timer expires before reaching the door/key

danger_signals.visual_form: Locked door graphics requiring acquisition of constellation seals or the main key
danger_signals.meaning: Progress blocked until specific items/conditions are met

danger_signals.visual_form: Different enemy sprite types with distinct movement patterns and behaviors
danger_signals.meaning: Varying threat levels and attack patterns requiring different avoidance strategies

safety_signals.visual_form: Placeable/destroyable orange cube tiles created by Dana
safety_signals.meaning: Player-controlled platforms for navigation, climbing, and blocking enemy movement

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectable icons appearing within levels or hidden rooms
safety_signals.meaning: Firepower upgrades, extra lives, bonus points, and seal acquisitions for progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Secret areas accessible through specific block-manipulation sequences
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones for bonus items, constellation seals, and alternative progression paths

safety_signals.visual_form: Greek mythology-inspired visual aesthetic with zodiac theming
safety_signals.meaning: Organized progression system; 12 zodiac constellations each containing 4 levels plus secret rooms

faction_indicators.color_identity: Primary character sprite, contrasted against environment
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Sorcerer character; summoner of orange blocks and fireballs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dana's position anchors the playable space; blocks created by Dana maintain consistent visual style

faction_indicators.color_identity: Distinct enemy sprite colors (varied per demon type)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Generic demon/monster designs; fireballs as signature attack
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple demon types with different visual designs, movement patterns, and spawn behaviors
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Enemy presence fills rooms; unlimited spawning indicates pervasive threat throughout Constellation Space

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Star-like seal of Solomon; the Key itself as central objective symbol
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Constellation seals hidden throughout levels; final objective is Solomon's room representing restoration of light

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cosmos presented as threat-filled puzzle space; enemies spawn continuously; Greek mythology aesthetic suggests otherworldly corruption
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies manifest as unlimited spawning threats; Dana must navigate chaos rather than defeat all enemies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Level organization by zodiac constellation (Aries through Pisces in sequence); each constellation group has 4 main levels plus secret room
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player advances level-to-level; finding constellation seals unlocks secret bonus rooms; mechanics remain consistent across constellations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Secret rooms and hidden items require specific block-building sequences to reveal
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must puzzle-solve to access hidden progression; Page of Time, Page of Space, and Princess Room only appear when all Seals of Solomon are acquired

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Solomon's room appears as final level; ending scene varies based on number of secret levels/seals found
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Completion of main levels allows access to final level; secret completion provides alternate/extended ending cinematics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Countdown timer prominently displayed during gameplay; visual urgency through time-limited access to keys and doors
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must balance puzzle-solving with speed; failure to reach key/door before timer expires results in level loss

environmental_storytelling.example: Greek mythology aesthetic (inspired by Jason and the Argonauts film) establishes tone of ancient curse/mystical peril requiring epic solution
12 zodiac constellations organize progression; implies cosmic/celestial scope of restoration quest; seal collection references ancient mystical knowledge
Orange blocks function as both narrative tool (player agency in reshaping space) and mechanical tool (navigation/enemy control); visual language unifies story and gameplay
Secret rooms accessed only through specific sequences reward exploration and experimentation; seals hidden in final rooms of each constellation encourage thorough play

environmental_storytelling.visual_example: Unlimited enemy spawning visually communicates persistent demonic corruption; enemies represent unending chaos until restoration is achieved

environmental_storytelling.example: Ending cinematics shift based on secret completion; narrative acknowledges player thoroughness; reinforces that hidden seals and rooms contribute to world restoration

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Can run, jump, create/destroy orange blocks, cast fireballs; movement responsive to player input; block creation/destruction adjacent to character position
Instant death; level reset or game over; no damage states or invincibility frames
Unlimited continuous spawning; movement patterns specific to demon type; collision with Dana triggers Dana's death
Animated projectile movement in arcs or spinning patterns; lethal on contact with Dana; can be destroyed by Dana's firepower
Act as solid platforms; can be destroyed by Dana or by hitting with head twice; persist until destruction; block enemy movement
Appears when timer permits; accessing it advances to next level; visual indicator of level completion path
Remain stationary in level; visible but may be hidden in secret rooms; collection upgrades Dana's firepower or provides extra life
Appear in final room of each zodiac constellation group only if specific puzzles/conditions are met; collection unlocks secret bonus levels

notes: Solomon's Key is a puzzle platformer where visual communication relies on minimalist NES-era sprite graphics supplemented by strong mechanical signals. Danger is conveyed through enemy variety, unlimited spawning, and instant-death mechanics rather than visual damage states. Safety manifests through player-created structures (orange blocks) and hidden power-ups rewarding exploration. The world-state progression uses zodiac constellation organization to structure advancement while maintaining consistent visual language. No faction-based color-coding exists; differentiation is purely enemy-type based. The narrative (demons released, Dana must restore light by obtaining Solomon's Key) is communicated entirely through UI elements, level structure, and ending variations rather than in-game cinematics or NPC dialogue. Environmental storytelling relies on mythological theming and seal/key symbolism.
